text: 67 km. north of Tandag City. The campus is considered the College of Technological Education. It offers ten (10) degree programs and eight (8) of which are accredited by AACCUP. The campus boasts of its buildings and facilities in Architectural Drafting, Automotive and Mechanical Technology, Electrical and Electronics Technology, Home Economics, Food Trades, Garments, and Computer/Information Technology also the state-of-the-art laboratories (Chemistry Lab, Biology Lab, Speech Lab and AVR). NEMSU-San Miguel Campus, the College of Forestry and Agriculture, is located at Barangay Carromata, SNAS, Municipality of San Miguel. It is the biggest campus in terms of land area with a total of 779 hectares divided into 3 physical contour areas: 379 hectares-the lowland; 175 hectares-the upland and 207 hectares-the forest reserves. Viewed on its scenic plateau, the campus is overlooking the biggest lowland rice bowl of the Province. The campus is identified by CHED as the Provincial Institute of Agriculture and Forestry (PIAF) under the NAFES program. With the present trend of Agricultural development, this college will cater the needs of the Province and the region as whole for skilled Agro-forestry workers. NEMSU-Cagwait is located in the heart of Poblacion Cagwait and a tricycle away from the Cagwait White Beach, a known tourist destination in the Province and the venue of the annual Kaliguan Festival. Its flagship program is Bachelor of Science in Industrial Technology (BSIT) with the following majors: Automotive Technology, Furniture and Cabinet Making, Civil Technology, Food and Garment Trades. In 2012, the program passed the preliminary survey prior to a Level I accreditation. NEMSU-Lianga is the Campus that specialized in Fisheries and Marine Sciences. In September 2009, it was identified by CHED as the National University/College of Fisheries (NUCAF) in CARAGA Region under the National Agriculture/Fisheries Education System (NAFES) project with a grant of P1M per year in 5 years. The campus has a notable Research Culture especially in Coastal Resource Management. It has experts in Corals, Seaweeds, Seagrass, Fin Fishes, Holothurias and Mangroves. Its on-going externally-funded researches on seaweeds and mangroves, its fish farming activities (Bangus, Tilapia and Crab) and extension programs along the coastal barangays of Lianga are proofs of how far the campus have gone in the area of not just research but extension. Most of its programs are already accredited. In 2010, its extension program gained national recognition when the Project Coastal Resource Initiative in Lianga Bay was awarded as Best HEI Extension Program in Caraga Region. NEMSU-Tagbina is the only campus located in the 2 nd District 2 of Surigao del Sur. It has a total land area of 215,418 square meters of which 77.347 square meters is utilized for demo farm which include the Bioreactor Technology Processing Center, Pastureland and Coconut plantation. SDSSU-Tagbina is the Campus for Agri-business and Commercial Sciences. It aims to lead in providing Agri-business and commercial education that will develop student’s entrepreneurial competence responsive to the needs of the agri-based industries. In 2012, six (6) of its programs passed the preliminary survey visit by AACCUP. It has also established notable linkages for research like DA-BAR, AFNR and DOST-PCARRD. NEMSU-Bislig Campus was formerly known as University of Southeastern Philippines – Bislig Campus, which took three years in transition until terms in the Memorandum of Agreement inked in 2018 came to an end in 2021. The Campus is located 14 kilometers Northwest of Brgy. Mangagoy and is within the allocated General Institutional Zone (covering an area of 50.7 ha in a 3.55 km Perimeter) per the 2017-2026 Comprehensive Land Use Plan (CLUP) of Bislig City. The campus would support human resources of Bislig – PAIC Industry Development Plan comprising light to heavy components of agri-based, agroforestry based, aqua-marine based, mineral-based, and footloose industry. Thus, BS Forestry, BS Mechanical Engineering, BS Electrical Engineering, BS Civil Engineering, and Technology courses have been vital to implementing the plan. In 2008, the Bachelor of Secondary Education Major in English was created in response to growing demand and increased opportunities to the far-flung area residents of Bislig City to take the course. In 2018, the Technology course was upgraded to Bachelor of Technical Vocational Teacher Education as part of the answers to the need for technical skills resources inside and outside the Caraga Region. NEMSU-Bislig aims to be the Center for Agroforestry, Paper and Wood Industry. Currently, five (5) of its programs passed the AACCUP Level II accreditation.

The history of the University dates back to 1982 when Bukidnon State University (BSU), then Bukidnon State College (BSC), opened its extension in Tandag City known as the Bukidnon External Studies Center (BESC). There were 153 pioneering students when the BESC opened its courses in AY1982-1983 with Jacinto P. Elpa High School serving as venue for the classes under the supervision of the late Rufina G. Luna who served as the first College Coordinator. In 1992, after 10 years, the Surigaonuns witnessed the birth of Surigao del Sur Polytechnic College (SSPC) with the signing into law of Republic Act No. 7377 on April 10, 1992 by the late President Corazon C. Aquino. The creation of the Polytechnic College through RA 7377 amalgamated the BESC in Tandag City and four (4) other DECS-supervised schools in the municipalities of Cagwait, Tagbina, Lianga, and San Miguel (formerly Tago) into one university system. In 1998, SSPC earned the status of being a state college by virtue of Republic Act 8628 promulgated in April 23, 1998. Dr. Antonio S. Ang served as the first President of Surigao del Sur Polytechnic State College (SSPSC). This development also led to the integration of Surigao del Sur Institute of Technology (SSIT) in the Municipality of Cantilan under the umbrella of SSPSC, further spreading the newly-established State College’s influence and services into the northern part of the Province. Dr. Ang’s stint as the President of SSPSC ended in 2001 upon his retirement from public service. This paved the way for the late Dr. Remegita C. Olvida’s appointment as the second President as approved by the SSPSC Board of Trustees in 2001. Dr. Olvida’s appointment as SSPSC President was renewed twice in 2003 and 2007. Her iron-clad perseverance for quality education and unwavering mission for the conversion of then State College into a state university was invigorating and inspiring. In 2010, through the efforts of the academic community, faculty, staff and students including the external stakeholders, with the support of CHED, other regulating agencies and the congressional district of the Province, by virtue of Republic Act No. 9998, signed into law on February 22, 2010, SSPSC was converted to Surigao del Sur State University (SDSSU). This changed in the status also extended the term of the late Dr. Olvida as Chief Executive Officer as she became the first University President of SDSSU now NEMSU. In 2014, Dr. Baceledes R. Estal, a former campus director, Executive Dean, and Vice President of Research and Development, was appointed as University President by the Board of Regents of SDSSU. Dr. Estal served the University and its constituents for two (2) terms until his retirement from service in 2022. In 2018, a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) with the University of Southeastern Philippines (USEP) in Davao City also led to the gradual turnover of USEP’s former campus in Bislig City to SDSSU (now NEMSU), making SDSSU Bislig Campus the 7th, the farthest and southernmost campus of the University. Many of the improvement and major changes in the physical facilities and façade, including the expansion of the University system to include the newly-integrated NEMSU-Bislig Campus, took place during the incumbency of Dr. Estal. On July 30, 2021, Republic Act 11584 was signed into law, renaming Surigao del Sur State University (SDSSU) to North Eastern Mindanao State University (NEMSU).

On July 30, 2021, Republic Act 11584 was signed into law, renaming Surigao del Sur State University (SDSSU) to North Eastern Mindanao State University (NEMSU). The university shall primarily provide advanced education, higher technological, professional instruction and training in trade, fishery, agriculture, science, education, commerce, engineering, forestry, nautical courses and other related fields. It shall also undertake research and extension services and provide progressive leadership in its areas of specialization (RA 9998, Section 2). A Research University advancing technology and innovation for sustainable development. We drive sustainable development through quality instruction, innovative research, community collaboration, and technological advancement. Compassion entails our value of promoting empathy, sincerity, and authenticity within the academic community to foster a caring and supportive environment where individuals feel understood, valued, and respected. Accountability signifies our unwavering commitment to integrity, honesty, and transparent practices, coupled with a sense of responsibility for our actions and decisions, ensuring ethical, effiecient, and cost-effective stewardship of resources for the common good. Responsive is a prompt action and release consistent quality communication that is focus on providing correct and complete action and/or information to clients and stakeholders. Excellence means our consistent pursuit of the highest standards of performance, characterized by innovation, dedication, and impactful contributions across all aspects of endeavor. Service is the embodiment of our professionalism, dedication, and a service-oriented mindset, committed to fulfilling our mission with excellence, integrity, and continual improvement, while fostering interdependence, collaboration, and sustainable success within the community and nation-building endeavors. These core values transcend mere descriptions of our tasks or the strategies we employ; they form the foundational principles guiding our actions and interactions. As we internalize our responsibilities to realize our university’s mission and vision, they serve as the fundamental elements shaping our approach to work and our relationships with clients and stakeholders. Moreover, they play a pivotal role in molding our students into competent, innovative, globally competitive, and service-oriented individuals. To develop competitive, resilient and transformative academic community. To produce high impact research and development for the advancement of knowledge and instructional innovation, institutional development and policy formulation, and commercialization. To improve the lives of the rural and urban poor through transfer of technology and knowledge. To develop and improve resource generation to sustain University operations. To deliver transparent, responsive and excellent governance for client satisfaction.

text: The Guidance Center Provides comprehensive and development program which facilitate the optimal development and well-being of every student cognizant to NEMSU's mission-vision. It is the process of assisting and guiding the person to resolve his/her personal, social, psychological, academic, career and vocational concerns. It leads individual towards better self-understanding, goal setting, planning and decision-making. This can be given individually or in group basis according to the particular needs of the students. - Individual Counseling - Group Counseling - Routine Interview Test provides “information in meaningful terms through quantitative description.” It is provided for the purposes of determining one’s personality – strengths and weaknesses, school ability, aptitude, interest and vocational inclinations through the aid of standardized test. - Intelligence Test - Admission Test - Aptitude & Interest Test - Personality Test It guide students in making and implementing informed educational and occupational choices. Through the different programs and activities students acquire the knowledge, skills, and experience necessary to identify options, explore alternatives and succeed in life. - Career Education - Career Fair - Pre-employment Seminar-Workshop - Mock-Job Interview - Job Placement - Orientation to New Students - Seminar/Symposium - Group Process / Structured Learning Experience The appraisal of how counselee’s who have been counseled, placed, or referred are doing, to determine whether further assistance is necessary. A coordination of multidisciplinary team of specialist to ensure that the student-client need is met. It is the tapping of agencies, organizations, or individuals that maybe of better assistance in the counselee’s resolution or creative management of the problems. - Peer Support System (PSS) February 21, 2017 10 Photos February 23, 2017 12 Photos

text: The NEMSU library system believes that relevant and up-to-date information sources are vital for the successful implementation of the instruction mandate, research agenda, extension, and production programs of the university. While the faculty, staff, and the students are the immediate users of information, the NEMSU Library firmly advocates in sharing these resources, for the scholarly needs of the people of Surigao del Sur and Caraga Region. The NEMSU Library shall provide resources and services necessary to support the curricular, instructional, research, extension, and production needs of NEMSU faculty, staff, and students, and in so far possible to share these resources in Surigao Del Sur and Caraga region in general. The NEMSU Library shall be the source center of excellence in various types of print and non-print sources in the fields of engineering, business administration, sciences, technology, education, social sciences, humanities, and management. It shall also be the information center of Surigao del Sur province. It performs an important role in the fulfillment of the four thrusts of NEMSU: Instruction, Research, Extension and Production. In line with the Vision and Mission of the university, the library shall achieve the following goals and objectives. - To provide sufficient, relevant, and updated information sources necessary to support the curricular, informational, recreational and professional needs of the clientele. - To contribute to the teaching role of the university and to support its learning and research activities. - To stimulate creativity and intellectual development among staff and students. - To organize, control, and maintain the collection in usable physical condition for proper and maximum utilization and effective library service delivery. - To provide bibliographic aids and assistance in identifying, locating, and using information sources including those not available, but which are needed to support the instruction, research, and extension and production activities of the university - To provide facilities and equipment for the storage and use of information resources. - To promote the use of the library and information resources. - To monitor, and evaluate library services and procedures to assure effective and efficient utilization and funds available now and in the future

text: including those not available, but which are needed to support the instruction, research, and extension and production activities of the university - To provide facilities and equipment for the storage and use of information resources. - To promote the use of the library and information resources. - To monitor, and evaluate library services and procedures to assure effective and efficient utilization and funds available now and in the future The history of the university dates back to 1982 when Bukidnon State College opened their extension in Tandag known as Bukidnon External Studies Center. The book collections of the library were taken from the main campus. Ten years later, on October 10, 1992 then President Corazon C. Aquino signed into law R.A. No. 7377 converting Bukidnon External Studies Center to Surigao del Sur Polytechnic College. Then, Surigao del Sur Congressman Mario Serra Ty and Ernesto Estrella jointly authored said Act. Surigao del Sur Polytechnic State College achieved its status as a university through House Bill 4411 sponsored by the Representative of the 1st District of Surigao del Sur, Honorable Congressman Philip A. Pichay. The Bill was signed into law as Republic Act 9998 on February 22, 2010. The creation of this learning institution integrates the first four (4) campuses namely; Cagwait School of Arts and Trade in the Municipality of Cagwait. Tago River Valley Institute of Agriculture in the Municipality of Tago, Surigao del Sur now San Miguel Campus. Institute of Fisheries in the Municipality of Lianga; and Barobo National High School in the Municipality of Tagbina. Eight years after, Surigao del Sur Institute of Technology (SSIT) Cantilan, Surigao del Sur was the 5th Satellite being integrated per BOT Resolution No. 34 Series of 2000. Recently, University of Southeastern Philippines (USEP) Bislig Campus was integrated as NEMSU Bislig Campus per House Bill No. 9432 sponsored by the Representative of 2nd District of Surigao Del Sur Congressman Johnny Pimentel. To reflect its geographical location in the North Eastern part of Mindanao and align it with the educational landscape of the region, thus, Surigao Del Sur State University (SDSSU) was renamed to North Eastern Mindanao State University (NEMSU) by virtue of R.A. 11584 dated 27th day of July, 2022. The seven campuses of the university have their unified library manual. Library services are now run by computers particularly in the technical services through Online Public Access Catalog (OPAC), which facilitates systematically the easy access of books in the circulation service. In the advent of technology, the Library Management acquired a Digital Library that contains eBooks, eJournals, eMagazines, Audiobooks, Videos and other online databases. The library trust fund is one of the financial resources utilized for the procurement of library materials, both print, non-print and other online subscription. In addition, the administration has also a budget for the library. Furthermore, the library explored and sustained strong linkages to add to the limited funds, thus, foundations, private colleges, and universities, local government units, and other government agencies, greatly contributed to the growth of the collection in the library. - Library Orientation and Instruction. - Book Search, Reservation, and Stack Service. Book Searching and Reservation of books are provided upon request particularly for graduate school students. Stack refers to old and complete issues of magazines and journals that are bound together for easy access. A school ID is needed in borrowing these materials. - Book Borrowing Students and faculty can borrow books and other library materials. - Current Awareness Services. Keep students and faculty aware of the new library resources and services through new book displays, bulletins, and others. - Bibliographic Service Upon Request. Listing of books and other materials on selected areas of interest is provided by the library upon request. - Study Carrel Service (Thesis and Dissertation) - Community Extension Service - Electronic Printing - Photocopying

However, a prescribed Library Permit Fee shall be required per entry. Payment shall be made at the Cashier’s Office and the Official Receipt shall be submitted to the library for service. Strictly overnight use of library materials is not allowed. - Proper Library Conduct The following general rules are to be observed when entering the library. Observe silence. Idle conversation, loud laughter, and other unnecessary noise must be avoided. In order not to annoy other library users, activities such as eating, sleeping, doing projects particularly for art education, accounting, engineering are strictly prohibited. Keep the library clean. Don’t litter tables or floor with waste paper and others scraps. Use the wastebaskets provided for the purpose. Keep things in order. Push your chair under the table when you leave the library. Handle books and other materials with great care. They are intended to serve many users. No student should ever commit such acts as hiding or stealing books and other library properties, nor tearing out of pages, or mutilating them. Books intended for use at the reading area only, must not be taken out from the library without the proper knowledge of the circulation in-charge. Bags, folders, big envelope, overcoat, umbrella, etc. should be deposited at the control counter before entering the library. Return catalog trays, books, periodicals and chairs to the proper places after using them. Do not pull out catalog cards on the catalog trays. Repeated violation of library rules and misconduct is a justification for the curtailment of the library privileges. Proper attire. Every student should enter the library with proper a attire. No wearing of slippers, shorts, sleeveless dresses and any cap or hat. Respect. Every student should respect library property as well as e each other’s right at all times. He/she should not write on n notices nor take away posted information and announcement on the Library Bulletin Board. A user shall seek written permission from the head librarian one day before using the library for an arranged service. - Validated Personal ID and Library Card - Complete School Uniform - Referral letter from their respective head librarian (Users from other campuses)

text: The program will produce graduates who are: - Highly competent marketing managers, marketing executives or marketing directors; - Business leaders who possess knowledge of the principles and concepts of marketing; - Business practitioners who organize or manage entrepreneurial activities if they decide to be self-employed; and - Willing to pursue a teaching career or graduate studies in business. The program will produce graduates who are: - Competent in facilitating the integration process in the corporate environment so they can be immediately productive once employed; - Equipped with complete understanding of the concepts, principles, theories and philosophies in Human Resource; and - Appreciative of the HR role in the organization and how they can make meaningful contribution as strategic partners in building the organization to become globally competitive. The program will produce graduates who are: - Globally competent in the field of business; - Business practitioners who serve as catalysts for socio-economic transformation; and - Business leaders who possess knowledge, skills and attitude in decision making. The college will produce competent graduates imbued with ethical values and ideals in humane environment supportive to national thrust for social transformation. The College is committed to: - Deliver quality and progressive education; - Engaged continually in research for innovation; - Involve in extension services and establish linkages with partner agencies and community; and - Develop competent entrepreneurs equipped with corporate social responsibility.

text: - Develop mastery of Current economic theories, principles , current best practices geared towards progress, development for inclusive growth; - To have an Adequate knowledge of the theories and principles as an end for conducting action, applied researches vital for discovering new to formulate economic concepts and principles relevant for economic progress and development; - Provide Strategies and frameworks designed to communicate effectively through teaching, research, production and extension works with the end view of providing a terrain in proposing solutions to economic problems. - To have a comprehensive understanding of the theories and principles of language for both in academic usage and its uses in the work setting; - To develop methodical and useful skills in language which can be revealed in communication discourse in the work setting here and abroad; - To hone strong sense of leadership where the skills in English language and communication are vital be it in the private or public sector. - To produce graduates who are vitally aware of the trends and issues in the field of public administration as an academic discipline and as practice. - Hone their skills in the field by providing avenues of actual work setting in order to prepare them for the work of work in the public and voluntary sectors; - Supply labor force in the field who are good instruments of accountability, and competence in the field imbued with good work ethics ; - Produce values oriented graduates with a sense of dedication in public service. - Provide an avenue in producing a politically and socially- conscious graduates who will be partners of public and voluntary sectors (nonprofit sector) in achieving developmental goals of the nation; - Produce graduates who are active players in the terrain of valuing the nation’s democratic processes and inclusive government; - Hone the Skills of the future leaders in the field for building a just, inclusive Philippine society in the global village perspective. - Professionally competent and trained in their chosen fields of specialization. - Skilled in dynamic leadership and effective decision-making. - Capable of integrating relevant knowledge in humanities and social and applied sciences, vital in a competitive job market. - Imbued with work ethics in a humane environment. - To be employed in government, private or self-enterprise where scientists with biological expertise are needed. - To undertake instruction in the tertiary level. - To undertake research in the various areas in biology. - Equipped with sufficient knowledge on the scientific theories and techniques needed to monitor and understand environmental quality. - Be able to integrate and apply the various disciplines towards the understanding of environmental problems. - Knowledgeable regarding relevant local, regional and global environmental issues. - Be able to employ a rational structured approach to solving environmental problems. As an academic base of the university, the College of Arts and Sciences is committed to: - Contribute to the progress of Caraga Region by producing specialists and high level professionals fortified with adequate knowledge and proficiency in the respective fields in social sciences, humanities, mathematics, natural and applied sciences,; governance, leadership and infused with sense of moral leadership, love of country appreciation of culture diversity and sustainable use of natural resources. - Adhere to acceptable standards in the conduct of teaching/instruction, thriving scholarly research, community extension and outreach programs, and production activities with the end view of producing workforce in fast changing “glocal” work setting. - Functions as a social laboratory that nurtures critical awareness of current/contemporary issues in both local, regional and ASEAN perspective, promote the sustainable use of natural resources for inclusive growth and effective linkage with partner agencies with the end view of quality instruction, research, publication and production.

text: The University’s Extension Program is an expression of its Corporates Social Responsibility (CSR). It is the door to direct social services that brings benefits of research to those in need; to share that knowledge of the application of the science to daily life for the less privileged people. This is in line with the development goals of PHER Roadmap 2010-2016 and the Caraga Region Agenda on FAME, Forestry, Agriculture, Mining and Eco Tourism that will contribute to a quality life. As one of important thrusts of the university, Extension Services was recognized as 2ndplacer in the 2008 regional level CHED Best Extension Program and as 2010 CHED Best Extension Program national qualifier. - To formulate and implement extension service in agriculture, fisheries and natural resources through locally, nationally, and internationally relevant extension program. - To train beneficiaries on technical and vocational skills training and engage in entrepreneurial activities based on needs, resources of the community and results of the product development research. - To conduct capability building for extension personnel, community continuing education for professionals, out of school youth, indigenous people and adults. - To strengthen health services, gender and development programs in adopted barangays and municipalities. - To establish partnership for fund sourcing to finance extension programs, projects and job placement of graduates from NGO’s and international funding agencies. by: Public Information Office | September 15, 2025 At exactly 6:17 PM, the #AMYzingFive of North Eastern Mindanao State University (NEMSU) College of Law triumphantly stepped out of the gates of Ateneo de Davao University – Senior High School (ADDU-SHS), marking the end of their 3-day battle in the 2025 Bar Examinations. What began in the early mornings, with courage as their armor and faith as their shield, has now reached its victorious close. After endless nights of preparation, years of sacrifice, and the unwavering support of their community, they have finally conquered one of the greatest challenges of their lives. Today, they walk out not just as examinees, but as inspirations that embodying perseverance, resilience, and the unbreakable spirit of future lawyers. As they exit the testing center, they carry with them not only the pride of their families and friends but also the hopes of the entire NEMSU College of Law family and the people of Caraga. Mabuhay ang ating #AMYzingFive of NEMSU College of Law! The exams may be over, but the journey to justice and service has only just begun. #amyzingfive #AMYzingBar2025 #nemsucollegeoflaw - Top News - More News Public Information Office | September 15, 2025 Public Information Office | September 06, 2025 Public Information Office | September 08, 2025 Cherra Mae Mangguilimutan | September 05, 2025 Public Information Office | July 30, 2025

text: by: Public Information Office | September 08, 2025 North Eastern Mindanao State University (NEMSU) strengthened its global linkages through a Virtual Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) Signing Ceremony with Fine Vintage Ltd., followed by an International Wine Talk, held on September 8, 2025, via Zoom Meeting. The university was represented by Dr. Nemesio G. Loayon, University President, together with the Vice Presidents, Internationalization Directors, heads, and other key officials. Fine Vintage Ltd. was represented by James Cluer, CEO and Master of Wine; Charles Curtis, Master of Wine and consultant based in New York City; Mike Lee, Director of Education Programs from British Columbia; and Ms. Jane Edorio, Sales and Administration Manager based in Tandag City. Fine Vintage Ltd., founded in 1995 by James Cluer MW, is an institution recognized for its work in wine education. It offers WSET (Wine & Spirit Education Trust) certification courses, wine training, wine tours, and consulting services across the Americas and Asia. The MOU outlines several areas of collaboration. NEMSU students and faculty will have access to free online WSET wine certification courses, with the possibility of microcredential recognition. The agreement also opens part-time employment opportunities for the top 50–100 students in sales and marketing projects, alongside internships in wine tour businesses that may be used as academic case studies. In addition, faculty members will receive specialized training in ICT, graphic design, accounting, and marketing projects. Both institutions will also explore joint research on wine production, marketing trends, tourism development, and other related fields. This partnership is particularly directed toward the BS in Business Administration (BSBA) and BS in Hospitality Management (BSHM) programs. It is expected to enhance academic instruction, research, and training, while also improving employability through industry-recognized certifications and practical experiences. Through this international collaboration, NEMSU continues to pursue opportunities that extend learning beyond the local context, ensuring that its graduates are prepared to engage with professional and academic communities worldwide. - Top News - More News Public Information Office | September 15, 2025 Public Information Office | September 06, 2025 Public Information Office | September 08, 2025 Cherra Mae Mangguilimutan | September 05, 2025 Public Information Office | July 30, 2025

text: by: Public Information Office | September 06, 2025 The Research and Innovation Office, led by Director Dr. Erwin B. Berry, and the Graduate School under the leadership of Dean Dr. Jennifer Montero, successfully spearheaded a 2-day Training on Thesis/Dissertation Advising, Paneling, and Publication on September 5-6, 2025 at the NEMSU New Board Room. With the full support of the University Vice Presidents and the University President, Dr. Nemesio G. Loayon, this initiative gathered key officials, graduate school faculty, visiting professors, and staff in a collaborative academic endeavor. The activity highlighted the institution’s strong commitment to strengthening research capacity and elevating scholarly outputs across its programs. Guided by the expertise of Dr. Arnold C. Alguno, Research Management Director of MSU-IIT, participants engaged in intensive discussions and hands-on learning designed to improve their skills in thesis and dissertation advising. The sessions also covered the intricacies of research paneling and the fundamentals of producing publishable scholarly works. This training emphasized both academic rigor and practical strategies, ensuring that faculty members are better equipped to mentor students and contribute to high-quality research. - Top News - More News Public Information Office | September 15, 2025 Public Information Office | September 06, 2025 Public Information Office | September 08, 2025 Cherra Mae Mangguilimutan | September 05, 2025 Public Information Office | July 30, 2025

text: by: Cherra Mae Mangguilimutan | September 05, 2025 The North Eastern Mindanao State University–Tagbina Campus was recognized as a Top Performing School after achieving a 100% passing rate in the August 2025 Certified Human Resource Associate (CHRA)™ Assessment Examination. With the examination’s overall passing rate of 81.86%, NEMSU-TC managed to surpass the standard through its two Human Resource Management (HRM) graduates, both first-time takers. The institution is among Batch 62’s 117 acknowledged schools that performed higher than the national average rating, with at least two first-time examinees. The Human Resource Educators Association of the Philippines, Inc. (HREAP) administered the Online Assessment Examination on August 23 and 24 for the Institutional and National batches, respectively. - Top News - More News Public Information Office | September 15, 2025 Public Information Office | September 06, 2025 Public Information Office | September 08, 2025 Cherra Mae Mangguilimutan | September 05, 2025 Public Information Office | July 30, 2025

text: In this momentous celebration, let us come together to give glory and praise to the Lord for the remarkable journey we have had during the Academic Year 2023-2024. Let us be gathered for the 𝐁𝐚𝐜𝐜𝐚𝐥𝐚𝐮𝐫𝐞𝐚𝐭𝐞 𝐌𝐚𝐬𝐬 (for Catholics) at the New Cathedral and the 𝐏𝐫𝐚𝐢𝐬𝐞 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐖𝐨𝐫𝐬𝐡𝐢𝐩 𝐒𝐞𝐫𝐯𝐢𝐜𝐞 (for non-Catholics) at the AVC-NEMSU Tandag Campus on 𝐅𝐫𝐢𝐝𝐚𝐲, 𝐉𝐮𝐧𝐞 𝟐𝟏, 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟒, 𝐚𝐭 𝟗:𝟎𝟎 𝐀𝐌. Graduating students, please wear your graduation toga during these ceremonies. The Praise and Worship program is set to be finalized this week. Catholic graduating students are requested to be at the New Cathedral by 8:30 AM to participate in the processional march and to secure your seats according to your program. This will ensure we observe the solemnity of the celebration with our very own 𝐌𝐨𝐬𝐭 𝐑𝐞𝐯. 𝐑𝐚𝐮𝐥 𝐁. 𝐃𝐚𝐞𝐥, 𝐃𝐃, who will preside over the Mass. Your presence - graduating students, parents, guests, and friends—is the most cherished part of this meaningful occasion. For this month, our Lead Mass Sponsor will be the College of Teacher Education. Together, let us rejoice, reflect, and renew our commitment to excellence and communal spirit as one NEMSU Family. Thank you and God be with you. 🤍💙 The Values Formation Office 🕊️ Tandag Campus May 26, 2024 August 13, 2023 January 27, 2023 January 25, 2023 January 25, 2023

𝗕𝗦𝗙𝗶 𝗵𝗼𝘀𝘁𝘀 𝟮𝗻𝗱 𝗣𝗿𝗼𝗱𝘂𝗰𝘁 𝗘𝘅𝗽𝗼 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗣𝗶𝘁𝗰𝗵𝗶𝗻𝗴, 𝘀𝗵𝗼𝘄𝗰𝗮𝘀𝗲𝘀 𝗶𝗻𝗻𝗼𝘃𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝘀 𝗼𝗳 𝗳𝗶𝘀𝗵𝗲𝗿𝗶𝗲𝘀 𝘀𝘁𝘂𝗱𝘀 When: | May 17 2024 08:00 AM - May 17 2024 05:00 PM Where: | University Brackish Water Fish Farm, Purok-1, Poblacion, Lianga, SDS Description: | The Bachelor of Science in Fisheries (BSFi) program of North Eastern Mindanao State University-Lianga held its 2nd Product Expo and Pitching on Friday, May 17, 2024 at the NEMSU Brackishwater Fish Farm. The event welcomed esteemed guests including 1st District Representative Congressman Romeo S. Momo Sr., Campus Director Mdm. Catherine F. Salomon, Dean of Forestry, Agriculture and Aquatic Sciences Dr. Clemencia Sumagaysay, Office of the Student Welfare and Development (OSWD) Head Dr. Ivy Orcullo, Department Chair Dr. Fabio Ruaza, BSFi Program Chair Henrietta Sanchez, and faculty from the Department of Fisheries and Marine Sciences (DFiMES). In his message, Congressman Momo complimented the students and the faculty for this undertaking. "I commend the students for their dedication and the faculty for unwavering support in nurturing these young and brilliant minds," he expressed. Mao ni ang gikinahanglan nato, ang mga estudyante na naay passion actually, to serve, to help, and to produce more," he added. Building on his visits to other NEMSU campuses, he expressed his hope to assist the campus with the infrastructure it needs and mentioned the possibility of showcasing the research-based products at the Surigao del Sur Provincial Capitol. The expo featured a variety of innovative aquatic-themed products developed by third-year students, including the Seafood Black Pasta, Tuna Pastil, Tuna Churros, Seaweed Pasta, Scallops Dumpling, Tuna Cupcake, Tunayaki, Seaweed Tuna Shawarma, Seaweed Pizza, Tuna Chips, Tuna Viscera Siomai, and Seaweed Tea. The fourth-year students also displayed four innovative research-based products: Seaweed Boat Tart, Dark Chocolate Tuna, Seaweed Lumpia Wrapper, and Seaweed Leche Flan. Additionally, a pitching competition capped off the event, allowing students to showcase their products and gain valuable feedback from a distinguished panel of judges: Rhea J. Santos, Food Technologist from the Department of Science and Technology (DOST), Paterno Silla, Jr., and Ms. Merlyn Dela Peña, both fisheries technologist processing majors, and Prof. Amor Rojas-Salinas, Chair of the Marine Biology program. Following the verdict, an awards ceremony recognized the outstanding products, where Seaweed Tea was declared the first place, followed by Tuna Chips in second place, while Scallops Dumpling secured third place and received the Best in Pitching award. Meanwhile, Seaweed Tuna Shawarma and Seafood Black Pasta both received consolation prizes. Organized by the BSFi Faculty Ms. Dian Dumbase, the event served as a testament to the commitment of the BSFi in fostering innovation and practical solutions for sustainability in the field of fisheries. | Report by Kaye Ann L. Quezada Photos by Jeszyrus Plaza and Mike Guarino #TheSeaStrivers #SailingAndStrivingForTheTruth #NEMSULiangaCampus

text: This is an ultimate cross-disciplinary research tool. It includes the Arts, Business, Health & Medicine, History, Literature & Language, Multidisciplinary News & Newspapers, Science & Technology and Social Sciences. Providing researchers with access to millions of scientific documents from journals, books, series, protocols, reference works and proceedings. AGRIS | AGRIS is administered by the Food and Agriculture Organization, a branch of the United Nations committed to fighting world hunger. The site contains millions of published articles, as well as an extensive collection of ‘grey' (or unpublished) literature from more than 100 different countries that focused on the fields like resource management, public health, economics, forestry, and crop management. Aquatic Commons | The Aquatic Commons is a thematic digital repository covering the natural marine, estuarine /brackish and fresh water environments. It includes all aspects of the science, technology, management and conservation of these environments, their organisms and resources, and the economic, sociological and legal aspects. BASE (Bielefeld Academic Search Engine) | BASE is one of the world’s most voluminous search engines especially for academic web resources. It provides more than 100 million documents from more than 4,000 sources. You can access the full texts of about 60% of the indexed documents for free (Open Access). BASE is operated by Bielefeld University Library. Bookboon | Publishes free and openly available textbooks and eBooks which can be downloaded in PDF. CIFT – Institutional Repository (Digital Library) | CIFT Institutional Repository is a digital service that collects, preserves, and distributes Institutional Publications(Journal articles, conference proceedings, Technical reports, Theses, Dissertations etc.) You can search, browse and access CIFT publications from this repository. Digital Public Library of America | DPLA is aimed at giving universal access to digital resources of American libraries and archives. Among contributing institutions, there are New York Public Library, University of Michigan, Harvard University, and the LIbrary of Congress. Digital Science Dimension | This is a next-generation linked research information system that makes it easier to find and access the most relevant information, analyze the academic and broader outcomes of research, and gather insights to inform future strategy. Directory of Open Access Books (DOAB) | It publishes free and openly available textbooks and eBooks which can be downloaded in PDF. It has 17070 Academic peer-reviewed books and chapters from 316 publishers Directory of Open Access Journals (DOAJ) | It publishes free and openly available journals which can be downloaded in PDF. DOAJ is a community-curated online directory that indexes and provides access to high quality, open access, peer-reviewed journals. Ebooks for all | More than 2000 free ebooks in English. On this site you can read online for free and legally digital books (eBooks) or download them directly to your PC, eBook reader, Tablet or smartphone. Edutopia: Open Educational Resources (OER): Resource Roundup | OER, a part of the global open content movement, is shared teaching, learning, and research resources available under legally recognized open licenses. ERIC (Education Resources Information Center) | It is an online library of education research and information, sponsored by the Institute of Education Sciences (IES) of the U.S. Department of Education. Free-Ebooks.net | This is a great collection of fun, entertaining selections. Categories include a few non-fiction topics such as career and business, but the selection of fiction and easy reading is what is most impressive about this website. Formats include HTML, PDF, ePub and Mobipocket, offering a range of variability for most digital reading devices. Infoplease | It is a vast compilation of statistics and up-to-date facts and figures on a broad range of topics. Internet Archive | This is a non-profit library of millions of free books, movies, software, music, websites, and more. It offers over 15,000,000 freely downloadable books and texts. Jurn | Search millions of free academic articles, chapters and theses. Library Philosophy and Practice (LPP) | This is a peer-reviewed electronic journal owned and published by the University Libraries of the University of Nebraska--Lincoln, Lincoln, Nebraska, USA. LPP publishes articles exploring the connection between library practice and the philosophy and theory behind it. These include explorations of current, past, and emerging theories of librarianship and library practice, as well as reports of successful, innovative, or experimental library procedures, methods, or projects in all areas of librarianship, set in the context of applied research. Marine Institute Open Access Repository | It facilitates full text access to the publications of the Marine Institute in accordance with copyright permissions. The aim of the Repository is to collect, preserve and provide open access to the publications of the Marine Institute, including the research publications supported by National and European funded marine research programmes. OMICS Group | More than 300 open-access scientific journals are housed in this database, with a readership of roughly 3 million users. Majority of these journals focus on life and medical sciences, dozens of others are concentrated in fields like pharmacology, environmental science, management, and engineering. Additionally, the organization sponsors more than 100 international conferences on an annual basis; these gatherings allow students to meet leaders in the scientific and academic communities, as well as faculty members and fellow students. OMICS Group | More than 300 open-access scientific journals are housed in this database, with a readership of roughly 3 million users. Majority of these journals focus on life and medical sciences, dozens of others are concentrated in fields like pharmacology, environmental science, management, and engineering. Additionally, the organization sponsors more than 100 international conferences on an annual basis; these gatherings allow students to meet leaders in the scientific and academic communities, as well as faculty members and fellow students. OnlineSchools.org | provides some of the most reputable open-access journal websites, as well as paid subscription databases that are still widely used by traditional college students. Open Library | The site is a project of the Internet Archive and is intended to create “one web page for every book ever published”. There are over 1,000,000 free eBook titles available. Most of the titles are public domain, but you can also find here a growing lending library of over 200,000 contemporary eBooks. Oxford Open | Oxford University is one of the many brick-and-mortar institutions currently making an effort to provide open access to scholarly publications authored by both current students and alumni, Oxford Open's database is comprised of archived content from more than 300 publications. All journals are listed alphabetically (regardless of their access status), and students may use seven filters (economics, humanities, law, life sciences, mathematical and physical sciences, medicine, and social sciences) to yield more subject-specific results.

text: the many brick-and-mortar institutions currently making an effort to provide open access to scholarly publications authored by both current students and alumni, Oxford Open's database is comprised of archived content from more than 300 publications. All journals are listed alphabetically (regardless of their access status), and students may use seven filters (economics, humanities, law, life sciences, mathematical and physical sciences, medicine, and social sciences) to yield more subject-specific results. Accounting and Finance Research (AFR) | This peer-reviewed journal (administered by Sciedu Press) features theoretical articles that cover various topics relating to the fields of accounting and corporate finance. American Journal of Economics and Business Administration | This quarterly journal has been part of the Science Publications (SCIPUB) canon since 2009. Articles from the most recent issue include ‘Retail Bankruptcy Prediction,' ‘Achieving Business Sustainability Via I-Top Model,' and ‘Single-Digit Inflation Targeting and Economic Growth. International Journal of Marketing Studies | Sponsored by the Canadian Center of Science and Education, this journal features hundreds of archived articles published between 2009 and 2012; IJSM initially appeared bi-annually, but is today published on a bi-monthly basis. The journal's central focus is marketing management and strategy, but each volume contains a wide array of specialized analyses. Journal of Entrepreneurship & Organization Management | This journal is still in its infancy; the inaugural issue first appeared in December 2012, and the site currently hosts five articles. However, the site features five additional “articles in press”; furthermore, nine forthcoming special issues will tackle topics like ‘Social Entrepreneurship, ‘Corporate Governance and Business Ethics,' and ‘Multinational Business Policy. Modern Economy (ME) | The journal covers a wide range of topics related to international economics, from agent-based models and exchange rates to sustainable development and trade patterns. Computational Intelligence in Neuroscience | This “forum for the interdisciplinary field of neural computing, neural engineering and artificial intelligence” features work from scientists, engineers, mathematicians, and other technology experts around the world. The journal's archive features nearly 200 individual articles dating back to 2007. Unlike most peer-reviewed journals, the CIN does not charge authors any fees to publish their work. Computer Science and Information Systems (ComSIS) | This journal (with an archive dating back to 2004) features both theoretical analyses of computational foundations and articles that explore the economic and educational aspects of the modern tech industry. The most recent issue (June 2013) features a handful of papers covering various topics, as well as two special sections devoted to intelligent information processing and IT in medicine and rehabilitation. International Journal of Information Security Science (IJISS) | Security science, engineering, and technology” is the central focus of this journal that has been published quarterly since March 2012. Journal of Artificial Intelligence Research | Considered one of the first exclusively electronic journals, JAIR first appeared in 1993. The site's archive contains hundreds of articles (housed in 47 volumes) that explore various trends, theories, and innovations related to the global AI industry. Journal of Information Security (JIS) | Published since 2010, the JIS examines the development, evaluation, and implementation of various online security models used to protect sensitive information and data. The most recent issue discussed security concerns related to mobile devices and identity authentication tools, as well as ‘2D Conjugate Maps of DNA Sequences.

Journal of Information Security (JIS) | Published since 2010, the JIS examines the development, evaluation, and implementation of various online security models used to protect sensitive information and data. The most recent issue discussed security concerns related to mobile devices and identity authentication tools, as well as ‘2D Conjugate Maps of DNA Sequences. American Educator | One of the most extensive journals currently available to web users, American Educator's archive contains more than 70 quarterly publications dating back to 1977. While the journal has historically covered a diverse range of topics, most articles are devoted to three central themes: technology and innovation in the classroom, evaluative methods used by educators, and student outcome trends and strategies. Current Issues in Education | Three types of articles are published in this journal: research studies, literature reviews, and outcome-oriented analyses. The bulk of archived articles take an ethnographic approach by exploring the relationship between educational trends, strategies, policies and gender, race, and socioeconomic factors. Early Childhood Research & Practice (ECRP) | First established in 1999, the ECRP is today considered one of the Internet's leading multilingual, education-based journals. Articles explore various strategies for teaching elementary school learners, as well as studies related to students belonging to specific demographics (such as special needs learners or low-income families). Advances in Chemical Engineering and Science (ACES) | This journal features articles that address molecular assembly, nanotechnology, and other concerns related to the development, production, and evaluation of various chemicals. The archive contains quarterly volumes dating back to 2011, as well as special issues devoted to supramolecular chemistry, advances in environmental chemistry, and carbon chemicals. Circuits and Systems | Established in 2010, this journal focuses on two of the most fundamental aspects of modern technology as they relate to computer-aided design (CAD), nanoelectronics, telemedicine, and other disciplines; short reports and literature reviews are also published. Journal of Bioengineering & Biomedical Science | This journal specializes in reports and reviews that discuss disease pathology, medical treatments, and health management. The archive contains quarterly issues dating back to 2011, as well as special volumes that focus on topics like regenerative medicine and emerging technologies in the fields of bioengineering and rehabilitative therapy. The Open Electrical & Electronic Engineering Journal | The articles, reviews, and editorials published in this journal cover a wide swath of topics associated with electrical and electronic engineering. Recent issues have explored x-ray detection, cooperative communication systems, network coupling, and duty cycle division multiplexing; in addition, a special issue released in 2013 was devoted to trends in signal processing and electronics engineering. The Open Journal of Mechanical Engineering | Published since 2007, this journal explores various problems related to mechanical engineering, such as industrial carbon emission output, longitudinal residual stress during friction stir welding, and stress and fatigue of vehicular engine components.

text: detection, cooperative communication systems, network coupling, and duty cycle division multiplexing; in addition, a special issue released in 2013 was devoted to trends in signal processing and electronics engineering. The Open Journal of Mechanical Engineering | Published since 2007, this journal explores various problems related to mechanical engineering, such as industrial carbon emission output, longitudinal residual stress during friction stir welding, and stress and fatigue of vehicular engine components. BMC Psychology | This all-encompassing journal publishes articles related to “developmental, clinical, cognitive, experimental, social, evolutionary and educational psychology, as well as personality and individual differences.” The archive features pieces that address topics like sex discrimination due to visual cues, culture-specific cognitive therapy techniques, and the short- and long-term effects of depressive symptoms. Child and Adolescent Psychiatry and Mental Health (CAPMH) | The archive of this journal dates back to 2007, and contains more than 200 research reports, case reports, editorials, and letters. CAPMH is geared toward psychologists, pediatricians, neuroscientists, and other individuals in the field. Many articles focus on niche groups of children and adolescents; recently published pieces examined the role of psychiatry within youth welfare organizations, explored treatments for young people with autism or Asperger's syndrome, and noted the ‘prevalence' of ADD and ADHD among children in Turkey. Evolutionary Psychology | Dating back more than a decade, this extensive journal specializes in articles and case studies that either bolster previous findings related to evolutionary psychology or seek to inform the public about emerging technologies. Many entries explore contemporary trends, such as drinking games and blind dates, as they relate to society and human behavior. Journal of Alcoholism and Drug Dependence | Addiction treatment and management is one of today's fastest growing psychology sub-fields. This journal (which debuted in January 2013) primarily explores various theories and strategies related to alcohol and drug dependence; recently covered topics include GHB addiction, ethanol consumption, and marijuana poisoning. Mental Illness | This peer-reviewed journal publishes editorials, feature-length articles, and case reports that relate to the diagnosis, treatment, and management of mental illnesses. Topics covered in the inaugural 2013 volume include the link between schizophrenia and violent behavior in children, mental health of male Internet users actively seeking sexual partners, and the effects of severe depression. - 100 Search Engines for Academics

text: by: Ms. Dina Mae Ibut, MSLIS | November 29, 2023 From November 21 to 24, 2023, seven librarians representing various campuses of NEMSU (NEMSU-Cagwait, NEMSU Lianga, NEMSU Cantilan, NEMSU-San Miguel, and NEMSU Tandag) embarked on a journey to the Culinary Heart of the Philippines—the Province of Pampanga. Their destination: the 𝗣𝗟𝗔𝗜 𝗡𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝗮𝗹 𝗖𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗲𝗻𝗻𝗶𝗮𝗹 𝗖𝗼𝗻𝗴𝗿𝗲𝘀𝘀, themed "𝗣𝗮𝗴𝗵𝗮𝗻𝗱𝘂𝗿𝗮𝘄 𝗸𝗮𝗴 𝗣𝗮𝗱𝗮𝘆𝗼𝗻: 𝗛𝗘𝗥𝗜𝗧𝗔𝗚𝗘. 𝗛𝗢𝗥𝗜𝗭𝗢𝗡. 𝗛𝗔𝗥𝗠𝗢𝗡𝗬." The congress unfolded at the SMX Convention Center Clark Field, Pampanga, 𝗳𝗲𝗮𝘁𝘂𝗿𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝟯 𝗞𝗲𝘆𝗻𝗼𝘁𝗲 𝗦𝗽𝗲𝗮𝗸𝗲𝗿𝘀, 𝟵 𝗣𝗹𝗲𝗻𝗮𝗿𝘆 𝗦𝗽𝗲𝗮𝗸𝗲𝗿𝘀, 𝟮𝟮 𝗣𝗮𝗿𝗮𝗹𝗹𝗲𝗹 𝗦𝗲𝘀𝘀𝗶𝗼𝗻𝘀, 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝟮𝟭 𝗣𝗼𝘀𝘁𝗲𝗿 𝗣𝗿𝗲𝘀𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝘀. Among the distinguished Keynote Speakers was 𝗔𝗺𝗯𝗲𝘁𝗵 𝗢𝗰𝗮𝗺𝗽𝗼, a Professor of History and the Humanities at Ateneo de Manila University. Ocampo, a renowned Public Historian, Independent Curator, scholar, and prolific writer, addressed the congress on the topic "𝑳𝒊𝒘𝒂𝒏𝒂𝒈 𝒔𝒂 𝑫𝒊𝒍𝒊𝒎: 𝑳𝒊𝒃𝒓𝒂𝒓𝒊𝒆𝒔 𝒊𝒏 𝒕𝒉𝒆 𝑨𝒈𝒆 𝒐𝒇 𝑫𝒊𝒔𝒊𝒏𝒇𝒐𝒓𝒎𝒂𝒕𝒊𝒐𝒏." 𝑹𝒆𝒇𝒍𝒆𝒄𝒕𝒊𝒏𝒈 𝒐𝒏 𝑯𝑬𝑹𝑰𝑻𝑨𝑮𝑬, Prof. Ocampo emphasized that being a public historian requires a collective effort, stating that libraries are torn between sharing and selfishness. On the theme of 𝗛𝗢𝗥𝗜𝗭𝗢𝗡, 𝗛𝗼𝗻. 𝗬𝗼𝗹𝗮𝗻𝗱𝗮 𝗚𝗿𝗮𝗻𝗱𝗮, Chairperson of the Professional Regulatory Board for Librarians, concluded in her keynote address that "HORIZON represents limitless possibilities, unexplored territories—the uncharted or unknown services still to be discovered in providing assistance to users and the transformative power of our profession." Granda encouraged embracing historical, technological, cultural, and educational horizons, paving the way for a challenging, more inclusive, and more enlightened future for LIS in the Philippines. A significant highlight of the event was the signing of an agreement between the 𝗣𝗵𝗶𝗹𝗶𝗽𝗽𝗶𝗻𝗲 𝗟𝗶𝗯𝗿𝗮𝗿𝗶𝗮𝗻𝘀 𝗔𝘀𝘀𝗼𝗰𝗶𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻, 𝗜𝗻𝗰. (𝗣𝗟𝗔𝗜) 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗣𝗵𝗶𝗹𝗶𝗽𝗽𝗶𝗻𝗲 𝗔𝘀𝘀𝗼𝗰𝗶𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝗠𝗲𝗱𝗶𝗮 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗜𝗻𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗺𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗟𝗶𝘁𝗲𝗿𝗮𝗰𝘆 (𝗣𝗔𝗠𝗜𝗟). This agreement marks a crucial step toward fostering a collaborative partnership, reflecting a shared commitment to the promotion and cultivation of media and information literacy in today's dynamic and information-rich environment. To foster 𝗛𝗔𝗥𝗠𝗢𝗡𝗬 and a sense of unity and shared enthusiasm, the 𝗟𝗶𝗯𝗿𝗮𝗿𝗶𝗮𝗻𝘀 𝗙𝗲𝗹𝗹𝗼𝘄𝘀𝗵𝗶𝗽 𝗡𝗶𝗴𝗵𝘁𝘀 were also part of the program. During this event, librarians and information professionals showcased their vibrant Filipiniana attire, adding a cultural touch to the congress's atmosphere. - Top News - More News Public Information Office | September 15, 2025 Public Information Office | September 06, 2025 Public Information Office | September 08, 2025 Cherra Mae Mangguilimutan | September 05, 2025 Public Information Office | July 30, 2025

text: by: University Library | November 25, 2022 The Philippine Librarians Association, Inc. (PLAI), in partnership with the National Commission for Culture and the Arts (NCCA) through the National Committee on Library and Information Services (NCLIS), and the National Library of the Philippines (NLP), conducted its Annual National Congress last November 22-25, 2022 at the Iloilo Convention Center, Iloilo City. This year’s Congress has the theme: “Working Together: Sustainability, Opportunity, Adaptability, Resiliency (SOAR)” NEMSU Librarians from Tandag, Cantilan, Lianga and San Miguel attended the said Congress. There were plenary sessions and parallel sessions sponsored by sectoral library associations to discuss topics relevant to the different type of libraries (i.e., academic, public, school, and special). - Top News - More News Public Information Office | September 15, 2025 Public Information Office | September 06, 2025 Public Information Office | September 08, 2025 Cherra Mae Mangguilimutan | September 05, 2025 Public Information Office | July 30, 2025

text: by: Melanie L. Marquez, RL | June 25, 2022 The North Eastern Mindanao State University (NEMSU) Librarians participated in the Tree Planting Activity (Arbor Day) last June 25, 2022, at Barangay Bahi, Barobo, Surigao Del Sur as part of the celebration of the PRC's 49th Founding Anniversary and PRC Week Celebration 2022, with the theme “PRC @49: Forging Ahead Towards PROFESSIONALISM, RESILIENCY, AND COMPETITIVENESS.” The participation of the PRC is anchored on PRC Resolution No. 1251 series of 2020 which declares the 25th of June every year as PRC Nationwide Tree Planting Day and makes it a part of the PRC Week Celebration. Pursuant to Republic Act No. 10912, otherwise known as the “Continuing Professional Development (CPD) Act of 2016”, an act which requires CPD as the mandatory requirement for the renewal of Professional Identification Card of all registered and licensed professionals under the regulation of the PRC each professional who actively participated in the event will be given with six (6) unit points. - Top News - More News Public Information Office | September 15, 2025 Public Information Office | September 06, 2025 Public Information Office | September 08, 2025 Cherra Mae Mangguilimutan | September 05, 2025 Public Information Office | July 30, 2025

text: by: Melanie L. Marquez, RL | June 24, 2022 In preparation for the upcoming ISO 9001: 2005 the Library Management of North Eastern Mindanao State University (NEMSU) conducted a two days NEMSU Librarians ‘Meeting and Workshop held at Big Times Beach Resort, Lianga, Surigao Del Sur last June 23-24, 2022 spearheaded by the Director of Libraries, Mrs. Aida C. Osorio with the approval of the University President, Dr. Baceledes R. Estal. The meeting/workshop begun with a prayer via multimedia presentation followed by a welcome address from Ms. Jehoney V. Alboroto, MSLIS Lianga Campus Librarian. Ms. Michelle Ann U. Ortega facilitated the workshop. The agenda were: 1) Revision of Unit Manual; Collection Development Policy Statement; Library Staff Development Plan and ISO documents requirements on the Processes: QOP, SOP, QOM, RR…. The focus of the discussion was the Revision of the Unit Manual. It was agreed that some forms will be deleted. The Context of the Diagram was also tackled during the workshop. Ms. Ortega created the Process Description which the Consultant required during the ISO Training at Grand Monique, Tandag City. On the second day, the meeting/workshop begun with a prayer via multimedia presentation followed by a recap of day 1 by Ms. Relina J. Balili, RL Cagwait Campus Librarian. Thorough discussions were made on the continuation of the meeting/workshop. Important topics were also discussed. The meeting ended at 5:00 pm with a photo op. - Top News - More News Public Information Office | September 15, 2025 Public Information Office | September 06, 2025 Public Information Office | September 08, 2025 Cherra Mae Mangguilimutan | September 05, 2025 Public Information Office | July 30, 2025

text: The University’s Extension Program is an expression of its Corporates Social Responsibility (CSR). It is the door to direct social services that brings benefits of research to those in need; to share that knowledge of the application of the science to daily life for the less privileged people. This is in line with the development goals of PHER Roadmap 2010-2016 and the Caraga Region Agenda on FAME, Forestry, Agriculture, Mining and Eco Tourism that will contribute to a quality life. As one of important thrusts of the university, Extension Services was recognized as 2ndplacer in the 2008 regional level CHED Best Extension Program and as 2010 CHED Best Extension Program national qualifier. - To formulate and implement extension service in agriculture, fisheries and natural resources through locally, nationally, and internationally relevant extension program. - To train beneficiaries on technical and vocational skills training and engage in entrepreneurial activities based on needs, resources of the community and results of the product development research. - To conduct capability building for extension personnel, community continuing education for professionals, out of school youth, indigenous people and adults. - To strengthen health services, gender and development programs in adopted barangays and municipalities. - To establish partnership for fund sourcing to finance extension programs, projects and job placement of graduates from NGO’s and international funding agencies. by: Public Information Office | July 30, 2025 𝐓𝐇𝐄 𝟒𝐓𝐇 𝐍𝐄𝐌𝐒𝐔 𝐃𝐀𝐘 | North Eastern Mindanao State University (NEMSU) has grown from its humble beginnings to become a thriving multicampus institution that is influencing the direction of education in the region. With campuses located in Tandag, Cantilan, Tagbina, Lianga, San Miguel, Cagwait, and Bislig, NEMSU continues to grow in influence and impact—serving diverse communities with a steadfast commitment to academic excellence and social transformation. Behind this progress lies an important moment in history: the enactment of Republic Act No. 11584 on July 30, 2021, which marked the transformation of Surigao del Sur State University into what we now know as North Eastern Mindanao State University. This legislative move was more than institutional rebranding—it was a declaration of purpose, a signal of new beginnings rooted in a proud legacy. As the university celebrates its fourth founding anniversary, it proudly carries the theme “𝑨𝒅𝒗𝒂𝒏𝒄𝒊𝒏𝒈 𝑻𝒓𝒂𝒏𝒔𝒇𝒐𝒓𝒎𝒂𝒕𝒊𝒗𝒆 𝑬𝒅𝒖𝒄𝒂𝒕𝒊𝒐𝒏, 𝑬𝒎𝒑𝒐𝒘𝒆𝒓𝒊𝒏𝒈 𝑪𝒐𝒎𝒎𝒖𝒏𝒊𝒕𝒊𝒆𝒔 & 𝑺𝒖𝒔𝒕𝒂𝒊𝒏𝒊𝒏𝒈 𝑬𝒙𝒄𝒆𝒍𝒍𝒆𝒏𝒄𝒆 𝒇𝒐𝒓 𝒂 𝑭𝒖𝒕𝒖𝒓𝒆-𝑹𝒆𝒂𝒅𝒚 𝑵𝒂𝒕𝒊𝒐𝒏.” This theme encapsulates NEMSU’s commitment to driving innovation in education, fostering inclusive community development, and maintaining high standards that prepare learners for the challenges of tomorrow. NEMSU stands not only as an institution of learning but as a symbol of resilience, renewal, and forward-thinking leadership. Its journey continues, with every milestone reinforcing its promise to empower individuals and uplift communities across Northeastern Mindanao and beyond. text: by: Arnold Rivera | September 05, 2025 ‎North Eastern Mindanao State University (NEMSU) Tandag Campus mentors placed 2nd Runner-Up in Painsayaw (Paindigay sa Sayaw) 2025 after presenting a performance inspired by the traditions of the Sama Badjao community during the opening of the 13th Sangkaan Festival at the Vicente T. Pimentel Jr. Memorial Gymnasium on September 5. ‎With the theme “Pagkabalaan sanan Pagkamalig-on Ipadayon, Para sa Dugang Kalambuan Kanato mga Tandaganon,” the competition featured five participating schools that staged cultural dances showcasing the identity, history, and resilience of the Tandaganon. ‎The NEMSU contingent interpreted Sama Badjao heritage through choreography, costumes, and storytelling to underscore the community’s cultural significance in Tandag City. ‎ ‎Jacinto P. Elpa National High School (JPENHS) won the championship and a ₱60,000 cash prize, while Tandag District 2 finished as first runner-up. ‎Photos by Hans Abordo - Top News - More News Public Information Office | September 15, 2025 Public Information Office | September 06, 2025 Public Information Office | September 08, 2025 Cherra Mae Mangguilimutan | September 05, 2025 Public Information Office | July 30, 2025

text: by: Public Information Office | September 01, 2025 Quezon City — September 1, 2025 The House of Representatives’ Committee on Higher and Technical Education (CHTE) is currently holding its Organizational and First Regular Committee Meeting at the Speaker Belmonte Hall, South Wing Annex, Batasang Pambansa Complex. Among the measures in today’s agenda is House Bill No. 2888, which seeks the establishment of a College of Medicine at the North Eastern Mindanao State University (NEMSU). The bill is principally authored by Surigao del Sur First District Representative Romeo S. Momo Sr. NEMSU officials VPAA Maria Lady Sol A. Suazo and Executive Assistant Mick Mars Silvano, led by University President Dr. Nemesio Loayon, are in attendance to express their support and readiness to provide inputs on the proposed measure. The university has conveyed its deep gratitude to the CHTE, chaired by Hon. Jude Acidre, for including HB 2888 in the committee discussions, recognizing it as a significant step toward advancing accessible medical education in Caraga and beyond. If passed into law, the measure will pave the way for the training of future doctors in Mindanao, helping to address the shortage of medical professionals in underserved areas and strengthening the delivery of health services in the region. - Top News - More News Public Information Office | September 15, 2025 Public Information Office | September 06, 2025 Public Information Office | September 08, 2025 Cherra Mae Mangguilimutan | September 05, 2025 Public Information Office | July 30, 2025

text: by: Rhea Malinao Cedron | August 30, 2025 Sa pagbubukas ng bagong taong panuruan, pormal na isinagawa ng North Eastern Mindanao State University (NEMSU) Tandag Graduate School ang oryentasyon para sa kanilang mga mag-aaral kaninang umaga, Agosto 30, sa University Gymnasium. Matapos magbigay ng pambungad na salita si Dr. Floresito D. Calub, ang OIC Campus Director, binigyang-diin ni Dr. Nemesio G. Loayon, SUC President III, sa kanyang mensahe ng inspirasyon ang kahalagahan ng pag-aaral upang hubugin ang husay sa kanilang propesyon. "We understand the vital role of the graduate school on your professional achievements, but foremost, that is very vital for your work and profession," ani Pres. Loayon. Kasunod nito, ipinakilala ni Dr. Jennifer M. Montero, Dean ng Graduate School, ang mga tagapag-ugnay ng programa, mga guro, at kawani ng NEMSU Tandag Grad School. Kalauna'y naglahad din siya ng mga balita ukol sa mga operasyon nito. Bukod doon, nagbahagi ang Vice President for Academic Affairs (VPAA) na si Dr. Maria Lady Sol A. Suazo ng mga akademikong impormasyon na may kaugnayan sa mga mag-aaral. Samantala, tinalakay naman ni Vice President for Research and Extension na si Dr. Rolly G. Salvaleon ang mga usapin hinggil sa pananaliksik ng Grad School. Habang nagpaalala ang University Registrar na si Ms. Lynnet A. Sarvida ukol sa mga dokumentong kailangang isumite upang maging maayos ang daloy ng kanilang pag-aaral. Kasabay ng nasabing orientation program, nagsagawa rin ng eleksyon para sa mga bagong estudyanteng manunungkulan sa NEMSU Graduate School Student Organization, kung saan pormal na nahalal si G. Mark Jade G. Burlat bilang bagong pangulo. Ayon sa datos na ipinakita ni Dr. Montero, umaabot sa 1,486 ang kabuuang bilang ng mga mag-aaral sa Grad School ngayong taong panuruan 2025-2026. Para sa mga karagdagang kuhang-larawan, maaring bisitahin ang Google drive na nakalagay sa post - Top News - More News Public Information Office | September 15, 2025 Public Information Office | September 06, 2025 Public Information Office | September 08, 2025 Cherra Mae Mangguilimutan | September 05, 2025 Public Information Office | July 30, 2025

text: by: Maisie Pecasales | August 30, 2025 North Eastern Mindanao State University (NEMSU)-Cantilan Campus formally launches the Infinity Binary Operation Game (IBOG) in collaboration with the Heirs of Cosme Y. Sering, sealing the initiative through a ceremonial Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) signing at the NEMSU Cantilan Hotel, August 29. This event highlights the NEMSU’s continuing pursuit of research, innovation, and sustainable development, with the IBOG project being hailed as a milestone in merging education, technology, and community engagement. In his presentation, Associate Professor Reinar A. Acevedo, project proponent and developer of IBOG, introduces the game as a mathematics-based board game designed to address learning gaps in rational number operations and common misconceptions about infinity. “I call it infinity because the highlight of this game is about infinity — a concept often foreign to learners. This board game transforms abstract mathematical concepts into a fun, meaningful, and engaging learning experience,” he explains. Key university officials, including Dr. Rolly G. Salvaleon (VP for Research) and Dr. Maria Lady Sol A. Suazo (VP for Academic Affairs), join representatives from the HCYS family in the launching ceremony. Their collective message underscores the university’s commitment to supporting projects that blend scholarship and innovation. “This initiative is powerful in purpose because it addresses students’ difficulties in learning mathematics. It proves that even the hardest concepts can be learned when introduced in a more meaningful and engaging way,” Dr. Salvaleon states, adding his encouragement to digitalize the board game to broaden its reach. One of the highlights of the MOA signing is the announcement of an annual ₱50,000 prize sponsorship from the heirs of the late Cosme Y. Sering. This pledge supports yearly IBOG tournaments, boosting the game’s popularity and creating opportunities for learners and enthusiasts in the surrounding communities. The event marks the beginning of IBOG’s development toward production and commercialization but reflects NEMSU’s vision of becoming a leading research institution that advances technology and innovation for sustainable growth. As the ceremony concludes with messages of commitment and a call for collaboration, the IBOG project stands as a symbol of how creativity, research, and partnerships can pave new ways of learning. Photos by CJ Lance Cubillas - Top News - More News Public Information Office | September 15, 2025 Public Information Office | September 06, 2025 Public Information Office | September 08, 2025 Cherra Mae Mangguilimutan | September 05, 2025 Public Information Office | July 30, 2025

text: by: Public Information Office | August 29, 2025 The presentation of the Revised Program of Receipts & Expenditures (PRE) is currently taking place at the University Board Room, 2nd Floor, Administration Building, NEMSU Tandag Campus. The activity commenced at 10:00 AM today, August 29, 2025, and is still ongoing. The University President delivered the opening message, underscoring the importance of sound fiscal planning and collaborative efforts in sustaining institutional growth. This initiative is aligned with NEMSU’s Vision of becoming a Research University advancing technology and innovation for sustainable development and supports the INNOVATE Agenda in ensuring transparent, efficient, and responsive financial management practices. In attendance are the Vice President for Administration and Finance (VPAF), Vice President for Academic Affairs (VPAA), Vice President for Planning and Strategic Initiatives (VPPSI), Budget Officer III, Administrative Officer V of the Procurement Office, Campus Directors of Tandag, San Miguel, and Cagwait, as well as the Campus Budget Officers and Campus Procurement Officers of the seven campuses of NEMSU. - Top News - More News Public Information Office | September 15, 2025 Public Information Office | September 06, 2025 Public Information Office | September 08, 2025 Cherra Mae Mangguilimutan | September 05, 2025 Public Information Office | July 30, 2025

text: by: Public Information Office | August 29, 2025 North Eastern Mindanao State University (NEMSU) made its mark in the recently concluded 2025 ASEAN Culmination Contests held on August 28, 2025, at Robinsons Place, Butuan City, bringing home multiple awards across different categories. From NEMSU Tandag Campus, the team of Johny E. Aporbo and Vaughn C. Chan, coached by Mr. Seberino H. Arriesgado Jr., secured 2nd Place in the ASEAN-inspired Fruit and Vegetable Carving Competition. Meanwhile, NEMSU Cantilan Campus clinched two awards. Reinon D. Rivera and Charles Justine L. Febrero won 3rd Place in the Dessert Display Competition, while Jeann Luengas Amasan and Romy Huelma Rosil captured 3rd Place in the ASEAN Attire Competition. They were mentored by Ms. Blessy Antido and Ms. Mary-Ann G. Geli. Equally commendable were the achievements of NEMSU Cagwait Campus, which also earned two distinctions. The duo of Rebicca C. Silvoza and Christine Mae B. Tagnipez, coached by Ms. Georgelyn G. Andrew, bagged 3rd Place in the Dessert Buffet Set-Up Competition. In addition, Alphon G. Ampilan and Cairel G. Omega, also trained by Ms. Andrew, secured 3rd Place in the Fruit and Vegetable Carving Competition. The ASEAN Culmination Contests served as a platform for students to display their talents while promoting ASEAN culture and identity. NEMSU’s consistent performance reflects its commitment to honing the skills of its students not only in academics but also in cultural and creative endeavors. - Top News - More News Public Information Office | September 15, 2025 Public Information Office | September 06, 2025 Public Information Office | September 08, 2025 Cherra Mae Mangguilimutan | September 05, 2025 Public Information Office | July 30, 2025

text: by: Jehoney V. Alboroto, MSLIS | March 21, 2022 The book selection and acquisition process are the responsibility and collaboration efforts of the Librarian, Faculty members, students, and other stakeholders to support the information needed for instruction. Since we still have restrictions on every transaction, book companies are trying to connect with us Librarians for easy and convenient transactions. With the support and approval of the Campus Director, Dr. Cynthia P. Sajot, the Assistant Campus Director, Dr. Bernardita G. Quevedo and Administrative Officer IV, Mr. Aniceto C. Sanchez, Jr., there were three (3) book providers who were willing to display their materials for an easy book selection namely: CD Books International, Inc., Serv Enterprises and New Century Books, unfortunately, only the CD Books had a chance to display their materials because of unplanned circumstances on March 21, 2022. - Top News - More News Public Information Office | September 15, 2025 Public Information Office | September 06, 2025 Public Information Office | September 08, 2025 Cherra Mae Mangguilimutan | September 05, 2025 Public Information Office | July 30, 2025

text: by: University Library | March 15, 2022 SDSSU Library Management System availed the subscription of the Digital Library with contents of E-books and Virtual Classroom , where users can access information both online and offline at anytime and anywhere from any devices - such as a personal computer, laptop, mobile, and tablet to ensure that users get information as where and when they need it with just a few taps on their device. This is a subscription of the whole SDSSU Library Management System of one (1) site per campus (each online resources). Each campus can enjoy a personalized web and mobile experience, with its own carousels and content. It is a user-friendly platform because it is fully inclusive, in all devices and has a text to speech and Open-Dyslexic for students with special needs. Digital Library with contents of E-books and Virtual Classroom is a NETFLIX-STYLED SOLUTION for INTELLIGENT DIGITAL LIBRARIES, it is a highly engaging, mobile friendly and personalized experience similar to Netflix. It has a Single Access to All Your Learning Content because it has a high-quality content of e-books containing 20,000 plus titles. SDSSU Library Management System can also upload its own existing library materials Digital Library with contents of E-books and Virtual Classroom Virtual Classroom - Top News - More News Public Information Office | September 15, 2025 Public Information Office | September 06, 2025 Public Information Office | September 08, 2025 Cherra Mae Mangguilimutan | September 05, 2025 Public Information Office | July 30, 2025

text: by: Ronie A. Sajelan, MSLIS | March 15, 2022 Library is considered "the heart" of the university. It has a prominent role to play in supporting higher education to fulfil the objectives of its parent institution. The role of the library is much more important since library is considered a prime requirement in higher education to facilitate the teaching-learning and research activities of the students, faculty and other stakeholders of the university. The function of library is to provide textbooks, references, periodicals, and other library materials on various subjects to students, teachers and researchers. Students in higher education are to be provided with the facilities necessary for mastering the subject matter, techniques, skills, habits of thought, and methods of work in their chosen field. Classroom instructions alone will not provide all the opportunities needed for attaining all these complex educational objectives. It is here that library come to help the students. However, the library cannot fully provide its services without a librarian. The presence of the librarian in the library can maximize its functions in providing necessary information to its user because a professional librarian has knowledge of a wide variety of scholarly and public information sources. The college librarian help students, faculty and staff in finding and recommending source materials as well as determining what type of materials they need. His duties include classifying, cataloguing and organizing library materials so that patrons can easily find and locate the information when needed. Recognizing this vital role of the librarian, Dr. Whelson Pasos, Campus Director of SDSSU Bislig campus requested Mr. Ronie A. Sajelan, the head librarian of SDSSU Tagbina Campus to extend his service to SDSSU Bislig as a shared librarian until they are able to hire one to manage their library. The said request was approved by Dr. Ariston O. Ronquillo, the Campus Director of Tagbina Campus, Mr. Ruel T, Lim, the HR head, Dr. Ramel Tomaquin, the Vice President for Academic Affairs, and Dr. Baceledes R. Estal, the University president. - Top News - More News Public Information Office | September 15, 2025 Public Information Office | September 06, 2025 Public Information Office | September 08, 2025 Cherra Mae Mangguilimutan | September 05, 2025 Public Information Office | July 30, 2025
